name: Mixpanel Analytics MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Mixpanel MCP server enabling AI agents to query Mixpanel analytics — events, user profiles, funnels, retention, cohorts, and JQL queries for product and behavioral analytics insights.
use_cases: Querying event data and user behavior from product analysis agents
Analyzing funnel conversion rates for product decision workflows
Accessing retention charts and cohort data via agents
Running JQL queries for custom analytics from agents
Querying user profiles for personalization agents
not_for: Teams using Amplitude, PostHog, or other product analytics tools
Infrastructure monitoring (Mixpanel is product analytics only)
best_when: An agent needs product analytics insights from a Mixpanel-instrumented product — usage patterns, funnels, retention analysis.
avoid_when: You're using Amplitude or PostHog — use those providers' MCP servers.

auth.notes: Service accounts for programmatic access (recommended); legacy API secret also supported. Service accounts provide project-level access control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 20M events/month, 60-day data history
pricing.notes: Generous free tier. Service accounts included in all plans. API access standard.

performance.rate_limits: 60 queries/hour for export API; Data API 5 req/s

known_agent_gotchas: Data queries have strict rate limits (60/hr export) — agents must queue queries
JQL (JavaScript Query Language) required for complex queries — agents must construct correctly
Date ranges in Mixpanel use project timezone — always specify timezone
Data can take up to 24 hours to appear — recent events may be missing
Service account has project-level scope but no finer granularity
Export API vs Data API vs Engage API have different endpoints and auth
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Service accounts with project-level access. Contains PII behavioral data — treat credentials carefully. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA. Good analytics platform security.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Service accounts provide better security than raw API secrets. PII data requires careful credential management. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA.
